install.summary: Open-source AI agent by Nous Research (MIT). ONE script-driven install builds the Electron desktop GUI AND puts the `hermes` CLI on PATH — same repo, not two products.
install.official_installer_DO_NOT_RUN: Hermes-Setup.exe (Windows) / the .dmg (macOS) / the terminal one-liner are a thin BOOTSTRAPPER that fetches scripts/install.ps1 (or install.sh) from raw.githubusercontent.com, then runs a heavy ~16-step build: uv -> Python 3.11 -> Git -> Node.js 22 -> clone repo -> Python/Node deps -> Electron desktop build -> Playwright Chromium -> PATH. DO NOT run it, and DO NOT decide what to do based on whether github is reachable: a responsive github.com does NOT predict success because most of those hops are non-GitHub hosts (PyPI, nodejs.org, the Electron + Playwright CDNs, plus the git clone) that get blocked/throttled independently. In mainland China it dies at the first hop (raw.githubusercontent.com GFW-blocked, os error 10054 / RST) or hangs mid-build (stuck at '0 of 0 steps'). ALWAYS drive the mirror-backed script install instead — see install_flow.agent_steps.

cli.note: The same install also yields a `hermes` CLI on PATH (oneshot: `hermes chat -q "<message>" --quiet`). Primary user-facing form is the desktop GUI; the CLI is a bonus, not separately maintained.

install_flow.summary: ALWAYS drive one mirror-backed script install — no network probe, no official bootstrapper. Set China package mirrors as env vars (Tsinghua PyPI; npmmirror for node/npm/electron/playwright/python), route git through a working GitHub proxy, fetch the install script via jsdelivr, then run it. Do NOT test GitHub reachability/speed to choose a path: reachability does not predict success (the build pulls from many non-GitHub hosts) and the official bootstrapper hangs whenever any one is blocked. This single path is robust in mainland China (primary audience) and works globally too.
agent_steps: Desktop app — LOCAL machine only. If connected to a remote server, switch to the local server first.

DO NOT probe GitHub/raw reachability or speed to choose a path, and DO NOT run the official Hermes-Setup.exe / .dmg / terminal bootstrapper. Reaching github.com does NOT predict success: the bootstrapper then pulls Python/Git/Node/Electron/Playwright from many separate hosts (raw.githubusercontent, the github clone, PyPI, nodejs.org, the Electron + Playwright CDNs); any one being blocked or throttled (mainland China — the primary audience) silently hangs it (e.g. stuck at '0 of 0 steps'). ALWAYS run the controlled, mirror-backed script install below — steps A-D. These mirrors are globally reachable, so this one path works everywhere; there is no branch to take.

STEP A — set China package mirrors as environment variables for the whole install session (stable, verified to exist): PyPI -> `UV_DEFAULT_INDEX=https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple` and `PIP_INDEX_URL=https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple`; uv's Python download -> `UV_PYTHON_INSTALL_MIRROR=https://registry.npmmirror.com/-/binary/python-build-standalone`; npm -> `npm_config_registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com`; Node.js portable -> npmmirror node mirror `https://registry.npmmirror.com/-/binary/node/`; Electron -> `ELECTRON_MIRROR=https://registry.npmmirror.com/-/binary/electron/`; Playwright -> `PLAYWRIGHT_DOWNLOAD_HOST=https://registry.npmmirror.com/-/binary/playwright`.

STEP B — route git through a reachable GitHub proxy so the repo clone (and any github.com release downloads) succeed: `git config --global url."https://<working-gh-proxy>/https://github.com/".insteadOf "https://github.com/"`. GitHub proxies (ghproxy-class) are volatile — pick one that currently responds. For PortableGit specifically you can instead use npmmirror's `git-for-windows` mirror (https://registry.npmmirror.com/-/binary/git-for-windows/) or just pre-install Git (`winget install Git.Git`).

STEP C — fetch the install SCRIPT from a China-reachable GitHub mirror instead of the blocked raw.githubusercontent.com: Windows `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/NousResearch/hermes-agent@main/scripts/install.ps1`, mac/Linux the same path ending `/install.sh`. jsdelivr mirrors GitHub raw and was verified to serve this file; if jsdelivr is unreachable on this network, use a ghproxy-class raw proxy.

STEP D — run the fetched script with the STEP A env vars set and the STEP B git proxy configured. The script's internal github.com calls are redirected by the git insteadOf rule; its pip / npm / node / electron / playwright downloads are redirected by the env vars. Watch the install actually PROGRESS — if it stalls on a network hop, read the log to find the blocked host and point that one host at its mirror above; never just wait on a silent hang.

BOUNDED LATITUDE + SECURITY: mirrors (jsdelivr / gh-proxy) come and go — if a hop fails, find an alternative working mirror at runtime rather than giving up; you may improvise beyond these steps. BUT the script you run is the OFFICIAL Hermes repo content fetched via a mirror — make sure the mirror serves the genuine repo (do not run a modified/untrusted script), and NEVER recommend a specific commercial VPN.
VERIFY: after install, `hermes --version` confirms the CLI is on PATH; the Electron desktop app is built under %LOCALAPPDATA%\hermes (Windows) / the app bundle (macOS).
Tell the user plainly what was installed (Hermes desktop GUI + `hermes` CLI), where, and how to launch it.
tell_user: That the install used the controlled mirror-backed flow (the official bootstrapper is deliberately bypassed)
That ONE install gives both the desktop GUI and the `hermes` CLI
Install location and how to launch
That China package mirrors / a GitHub proxy were used so the build could complete

network_requirements.note: These hosts are what the build TOUCHES — NOT a checklist to probe-and-gate on. Do NOT decide the strategy by testing them; install_flow above is authoritative and ALWAYS uses mirrors. Listed only so you know which host to redirect to which mirror if a hop stalls.

blocked_regions.zh-CN: The official Hermes-Setup.exe is a bootstrapper that fetches scripts/install.ps1 from raw.githubusercontent.com — ~always GFW-blocked in mainland China (os error 10054 / RST), so the official installer fails outright. The build also pulls from github.com (clone, PortableGit), PyPI, nodejs.org, npm, the Electron CDN and the Playwright CDN — most are throttled/blocked in CN. Bypass: fetch the script via jsdelivr/ghproxy and set China package mirrors (Tsinghua PyPI; npmmirror for node/npm/electron/playwright/python-build-standalone; a GitHub proxy for the git clone). See install_flow.agent_steps.

mirrors.github_raw: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/NousResearch/hermes-agent@main/ (jsdelivr; or a ghproxy-class raw proxy, volatile — verify at runtime)
mirrors.github_clone: git insteadOf via a ghproxy-class proxy (volatile — verify at runtime)
